summary.intent: Address PR review comments on PR #505 in the moltis repo by fixing a test assertion and correcting the role used for compaction summary messages.
summary.outcome: Changed compaction summary messages from `PersistedMessage::System` to `PersistedMessage::User` to avoid silent data loss in the Responses API path, strengthened test assertions to positive `System` matches, and pushed a follow-up commit resolving all review conversations.

repo: The Responses API path (`to_responses_input()`) merges `System` messages into `instructions` and drops them from the turn array — any data stored as a System message is silently lost from turn history.
Two consecutive `User` messages (e.g., compaction summary followed by the next user question) are valid across all supported providers (Chat Completions and Responses API).
`MessageContent` is imported at the crate level in `crates/chat/src/lib.rs`, making it available to free functions like `compact_session` without additional imports.

code.finding: Both compaction sites were updated to emit `PersistedMessage::User` instead of `PersistedMessage::System` to ensure the compaction summary survives the Responses API path.
`to_responses_input()` silently drops System-role messages by merging them into `instructions`, confirmed by code inspection during the review fix.

workflow: Running the full test suite (151 tests) after targeted edits confirmed no regressions before pushing the fix.
Review comments were addressed in a single focused commit that covered both the behavioral fix and the test assertion improvements.

friction: Required multiple grep passes to confirm `MessageContent` import scope before being confident the edit was safe.
The Responses API silent-drop behavior was a non-obvious gotcha that required reading `to_responses_input()` source to validate the reviewer's concern.
